name: wisp
readme: # wisp - a lightweight nostr relay A fast, lightweight [nostr](https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nostr) relay written in Zig. ## Why Wisp? - **Fast**: 2x higher throughput than strfry, 10x lower latency - **Small**: Single 1.2MB binary, ~15MB RAM at idle - **Simple**: One command to run your personal relay with your feed - **Spider Mode**: Automatically syncs events from people you follow ## Quickstart Download the [latest release](https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/releases) or build from source: ```sh # 1. Install dependencies (requires Zig 0.15+) sudo apt install -y liblmdb-dev libsecp256k1-dev libssl-dev # 2. Build git clone https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp && cd wisp zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseFast # 3. Run (replace with your npub) ./zig-out/bin/wisp --spider-admin npub1yourkey... ``` That's it. Wisp will fetch your follow list, sync notes from popular relays, and serve them at `ws://localhost:7777`. Add this relay URL to your Nostr client. For more options, see `wisp.toml.example`. ## Features * NIPs: 1, 2, 9, 11, 13, 16, 22, 33, 40, 42, 45, 50, 65, 70, 77, 86 * LMDB storage (no external database) * Spider mode for syncing events from external relays * Import/export to JSONL ## Import/Export ```sh ./zig-out/bin/wisp export > backup.jsonl ./zig-out/bin/wisp import < backup.jsonl ``` ## Configuration Copy `wisp.toml.example` to `wisp.toml` and customize, or use environment variables with `WISP_` prefix: ```sh WISP_PORT=8080 ./zig-out/bin/wisp ``` See `wisp.toml.example` for all options. ## Deploy (wss://) To make your relay publicly accessible with TLS, put Caddy in front of wisp: ```sh # Install Caddy sudo apt install -y caddy ``` Create `/etc/caddy/Caddyfile`: ``` relay.yourdomain.com { reverse_proxy localhost:7777 } ``` Create `/etc/systemd/system/wisp.service`: ```ini [Unit] Description=Wisp Nostr Relay After=network.target [Service] ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/wisp --spider-admin npub1yourkey... WorkingDirectory=/var/lib/wisp Restart=always User=wisp [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target ``` ```sh # Set up and start sudo useradd -r -s /bin/false wisp sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/wisp sudo chown wisp:wisp /var/lib/wisp sudo cp zig-out/bin/wisp /usr/local/bin/ sudo systemctl enable --now wisp caddy ``` Add `trust_proxy = true` to your `wisp.toml` if using rate limiting behind Caddy. Your relay is now live at `wss://relay.yourdomain.com`. ## Benchmarks | Relay | Events/sec | p50 Latency | p99 Latency | |-------|-----------|-------------|-------------| | Wisp | 1,993 | 0.55ms | 0.82ms | | strfry | 872 | 4.28ms | 9.92ms | *4 concurrent workers, 1k events. [Full results](https://github.com/privkeyio/nostr-bench/blob/main/reports/BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md)* ## Contributing See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines on reporting issues and submitting pull requests. ## Changelog See [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) for a history of changes to this project. ## License MIT

releases.name: v0.2.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Update benchmark results by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/31
* Fix config file argument parsing and inline comment handling by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/32
* Update benchmark results and fix spider connection handling by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/33
* Update libnostr-z to v0.1.6 by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/35
* Bug Fixes by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/38
* Add Changelog and issue policy by @wksantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/37
* Non-blocking write queue with per-connection worker threads by @wksantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/48
* Add NIP-86 management API support by @wksantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/36
* Add NIP-22 to supported NIPs by @wksantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/47
* License/mit by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/53
* Update websocket dependency hash by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/54
* Security hardening & Bump to v0.2.0 by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/55


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/compare/v0.1.2...v0.2.0

releases.name: v0.1.2
releases.body:
Update libnostr-z to v0.1.5

## What's Changed
* Update libnostr-z to v0.1.5 by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/28
* Bump version to 0.1.2 by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/29
* Update libnostr-z to v0.1.5 with macOS ARM64 fix by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/30


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/compare/v0.1.1...v0.1.2

releases.name: v0.1.1
releases.body:
- Add NIP-13 proof of work support
- Add NIP-65 relay list metadata support
- Add NIP-70 protected events support

## What's Changed
* Update libnostr-z to v0.1.4 by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/26
* Remove manual noscrypt clone from CI by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/27
* Add NIP-70 support by @wksantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/15
* Add NIP-65 support by @wksantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/23
* Add NIP-13 support by @wksantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/25


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/compare/v0.1.0...v0.1.1

releases.name: v0.1.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* feat: Add NIP-77 negentropy sync support by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/12
* feat: NIP-50 Support by @wksantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/4
* Update nostr dependency hash for root.zig rename by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/13
* Bug Fixes, update libnostr-z to v0.1.3, add quickstart, add benchmark CI by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/14

## New Contributors
* @kwsantiago made their first contribution in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/12
* @wksantiago made their first contribution in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/4

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/commits/v0.1.0

owner_bio: PrivKey is an enterprise cybersecurity firm specializing in Bitcoin, Nostr & Lightning Network infrastructure and institutional-grade digital asset security.
